{
  "risk": "HIGH",
  "riskReason": "Database state mutation during validation queries and potential race condition in BlockedEmail.should_block? can cause data corruption and unpredictable behavior.",
  "circularTests": {
    "detected": false,
    "count": 0,
    "total": 2,
    "details": ""
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Data Mutation During Validation",
      "file": "app/models/blocked_email.rb",
      "line": 13,
      "title": "BlockedEmail.should_block? mutates database state during validation",
      "description": "The should_block? method increments match_count and updates last_match_at, then saves the record. This happens during email validation, which is called multiple times per request (form validation, submission, etc.). This causes unintended side effects: match counters get incremented on every validation pass, not just actual registration attempts. The method should read-only during validation, with mutations deferred to explicit action handlers.",
      "suggestion": "Separate concerns: create a read-only should_block? method that only queries the BlockedEmail record, and a separate record_block_match(email) method that handles increment/save operations. Call the latter only after successful or failed user creation, not during validation."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Race Condition",
      "file": "app/models/blocked_email.rb",
      "line": 11,
      "title": "Race condition in BlockedEmail.should_block? read-modify-write",
      "description": "Lines 11-16 perform a check-then-act pattern without locking: query record, modify match_count/last_match_at, save. Between the query and save, another request could modify the same record, causing lost updates. In high-concurrency scenarios, match_count increments will be lost.",
      "suggestion": "Use database-level atomic operations: `BlockedEmail.where(email: email).update_all('match_count = match_count + 1, last_match_at = ?', Time.zone.now)` to atomically increment, or wrap in a transaction with row-level locking using `find_with_lock`."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Incomplete Validation Migration",
      "file": "app/models/user.rb",
      "line": 48,
      "title": "Removed email domain whitelist/blacklist validation not replicated in new validator",
      "description": "The email_validator method is removed from User model and its logic should move to lib/validators/email_validator.rb. However, the whitelist/blacklist logic is duplicated in EmailValidator, but there's no guarantee it's called. The validates :email, email: true on line 48 relies on ActiveModel finding the EmailValidator class by convention, which is fragile. If the validator isn't properly registered, domain restrictions silently disappear.",
      "suggestion": "Explicitly pass the validator class: `validates :email, email_validator: true` or use `EmailValidator.new()` with custom configuration. Add an integration test confirming whitelist/blacklist restrictions still work end-to-end."
    }
  ]
}
